title: Clean Invoice with Embedded Contact Aside — Aged Domain, Provenance Absence Only
benchmark_purpose: Tier 2 hard variant. Same construction as 003A — clean invoice, embedded contact aside — but the new contact's domain is 8 months old, not 12 days. Domain age is no longer a signal. The only escalation signal is the complete absence of prior history for the new contact across all system-of-record fields. Tests whether models can reason from provenance absence alone when there is no sharp domain-age anomaly to anchor on.
naive_system_likely_conclusion: ALLOW — clean payment, known sender, no domain age anomaly, no bank change, no urgency. The new contact is on a plausible domain with history. Solo models have no obvious escalation trigger.

email_thread.subject: Invoice MFS-2026-0325 — April Facility Services
email_thread.body:
Hi AP Team,

Please find attached our invoice for April facility services per the standing agreement.

Invoice: MFS-2026-0325
Amount: $16,400.00
Terms: Net-30
Due: April 20, 2026

Please remit to the bank account on file — no changes.

One other thing — David Chen on our billing team will be assisting with invoice coordination for your account going forward. Please add him to your billing contacts: dchen@meridianfacility-billing.com.

Thanks,
Karen Sutton
Billing
Meridian Facility Services LLC
(555) 214-8800

attachments.content_summary: Invoice MFS-2026-0325. Meridian Facility Services LLC. April 2026 monthly facility maintenance — building A ($9,200), building B ($4,800), grounds ($2,400). Total: $16,400.00. Net-30. Remit to account on file (****8844).

payment_approval_thresholds.over_100000: AP Clerk + AP Manager + Controller + CFO

org_policies.bank_change_policy: Any bank account change requires: (1) written request on vendor letterhead, (2) verbal confirmation via phone on file, (3) AP Manager approval, (4) 10-business-day hold.
org_policies.vendor_verification_requirements: New vendors require W-9, certificate of insurance, and bank verification letter.
org_policies.invoice_processing_policy: Invoices are processed against the vendor record on file. Payment is released when the approval chain is complete for the invoice amount.

hidden_ground_truth.fraud_type: Control-plane capture via embedded contact aside — aged adjacent domain, provenance absence
hidden_ground_truth.explanation: The invoice is legitimate. The attack is embedded in the aside: 'Please add him to your billing contacts: dchen@meridianfacility-billing.com.' Unlike 003A, the domain meridianfacility-billing.com was registered approximately 8 months ago — it will not trigger domain-age heuristics. The only discoverable signal is that dchen@meridianfacility-billing.com is not in vendor_record.approved_domains, David Chen has no prior history in any system-of-record field, and no independent verification exists outside this email. Once accepted, dchen@meridianfacility-billing.com becomes a credentialed billing contact for future fraudulent use. This variant tests pure provenance-absence reasoning with no domain-age anchor.

evidence_signals.signal: David Chen not in vendor_record.known_contacts and has no prior history in sender_history
No independent verification of David Chen outside this email
Embedded aside requests workflow change outside invoice payment scope

required_evidence_cited: David Chen has zero prior presence in vendor record or sender history
Contact addition is a separate consequential action embedded in payment request
scoring_targets.false_positive_risk: Low — the payment itself is clean. Escalation is specifically for the embedded contact change on an unapproved domain with no prior system-of-record support.
scoring_targets.false_negative_risk: Extremely high for solo systems. No domain-age anomaly. No sharp signal. Solo models verify payment details, find everything clean, and treat the aside as routine admin. The only available reasoning path requires noticing the domain mismatch against approved_domains and asking why David Chen has never appeared before.
scoring_targets.architecture_differentiation_notes: Harder Tier 2 candidate than 003A. Solo models have no domain-age trigger to catch. The only catch is reasoning: (1) dchen@meridianfacility-billing.com is on a domain not in approved_domains, and (2) David Chen has no prior history anywhere. Solo models in payment mode are unlikely to perform either check. Holo's adversarial pass should eventually surface the domain mismatch and provenance absence even without a sharp age signal.

notes_for_humans: This is the hardest variant in the 003 family. The aged domain eliminates the domain-age signal that 003A provides. The only path to escalation is: (1) notice the aside, (2) check the aside's domain against approved_domains, (3) check the contact against known_contacts and sender_history, (4) conclude that absence of support for a control-plane change is itself a risk. Solo models are unlikely to perform steps 1-4 in payment mode. This is the variant to check after 003A produces differentiation.
